Checking the Cursor settings skill and how display language is configured in this environment.

input.command: where cursor 2>$null; if (Get-Command cursor -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) { cursor --help 2>&1 | Select-Object -First 40 }
input.description: Locate Cursor CLI and show help for locale flags

input.command: Get-ChildItem -Path "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\cursor" -Filter "Cursor.exe" -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -First 3 -ExpandProperty FullName; Get-ChildItem -Path "$env:ProgramFiles\Cursor" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
input.description: Find Cursor.exe installation path on Windows

content.text:
Here is how **Display Language** works in Cursor (same model as VS Code) and what you can do next.

## In the UI (recommended)

1. Press **Ctrl+Shift+P** to open the Command Palette.
2. Run **Configure Display Language** (or **Display Language: Configure Display Language**).
3. Pick a language. If yours is missing, choose **Install additional languages…** and install the matching **Language Pack** from Extensions, then run the command again and select it.
4. **Restart Cursor** when prompted so the UI reloads in that language.

## File-based configuration (optional)

Cursor reads **`%APPDATA%\Cursor\User\locale.json`** (on your machine: `C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Roaming\Cursor\User\locale.json`).  
That file is **not** the same as `settings.json`; display language is controlled here, not under editor settings.

Typical shape:

```json
{
  "locale": "zh-cn"
}
```

Use the same locale id Cursor/VS Code shows in the language list (examples: `en`, `de`, `fr`, `es`, `ja`, `zh-cn`, `zh-tw`, `ko`, `pt-br`, `ru`).

You still need the corresponding **Language Pack** extension installed for any language other than the default English UI.

---

**Note:** `settings.json` does not control display language; the update-cursor-settings flow applies to editor/workbench options, not UI locale.

You did not specify which language you want. If you tell me the **exact language** (and, if you know it, the **locale id** from the list), I can give you the precise `locale.json` contents for your choice—or you can use the Command Palette steps above without editing files.

[REDACTED]
